DECLARATION OF DAVID R. DONADIO PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36.5 IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (TRANSACTION ID # 76962447) FILED BY PLAINTIFF HILLS, RONALD

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE TRIAL DATE IS JANUARY 20, 2026, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS JANUARY 28, 2026. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS JANUARY 13, 2026. SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS SHALL BE BROUGHT ON REGULAR NOTICE PURSUANT TO THE RELEVANT PROVISIONS OF THE C.C.P., UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE OTHERWISE. BEFORE A PARTY FILES AND SERVES A SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTION, IT MUST CONTACT THE CLERK TO MAKE A RESERVATION. THE COURT ALLOWS A MAXIMUM OF FOUR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS PER DAY TO BE CALENDARED, UNLESS GOOD CAUSE IS FOUND TO EXCEED THIS NUMBER. CONTACT THE CLERK TO SCHEDULE A GOOD CAUSE HEARING. ELECTRONIC SERVICE IS CONSIDERED THE EQUIVALENT OF PERSONAL SERVICE. THE FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS JANUARY 2, 2026. THE EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS JANUARY 16, 2026. ALL BANKRUPTCY DOCUMENTS SHALL BE TURNED OVER TO DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 30, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR DECEMBER 2, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENTS ARE NOT REQUIRED. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY ZOOM. IF THE PARTIES AGREE THAT NO CMC IS NECESSARY, THEY SHALL NOTIFY THE COURT AT LEAST TWO COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 15, 2026, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS EXCUSED FOR GOOD CAUSE. A REQUEST TO EXCUSE A PERSONAL APPEARANCE MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT503@SFTC.ORG TWO WEEKS BEFORE THE MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. REPORTER: NOT REPORTED (EPS/304).

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar Sep-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (mini-minutes 2 of 2)

8. No later than October 14, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
9. A further case management conference is set for December 2, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than December 8, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due December 22, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on December 29, 2025.
11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, January 15, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Johnny Sengmany. Reporter: NOT REPORTED (EPS/304).

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Sep-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 30, 2025, in Department 304, Line 2.

No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed.
1. The trial date is January 20, 2026, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is January 28, 2026.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is January 13, 2026.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is January 2, 2026.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is January 16, 2026.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 30, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(mini-minutes 1 of 2 continues, see next entry)
